source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/11_Audit F-up/240415-17 ISO 갱신심사 & 2,3G MDR 심사 - 3EC/MDR 보완/[2nd response TD by H&abyz] Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector (08 March 2024) word/A-TCF-5-157 Clinical Evaluation/A-TCF-5-120 Literature Study (rev.2).docx

document.title: Literature Study
document.documentType: Literature search protocol and literature search report
authorityOrJurisdiction: MFDS Product recall and Suspension of Sale (Korea)
MHRA Alerts and recalls for drugs and medical devices (UK)
standards: Medical Device Regulation (EU) 2017/745 Annex VIII Rule 10

sourceSummary.oneLine: Literature study for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW and F1417MCW covering literature search protocol, clinical literature appraisal, equivalence-related devices, and recall searches.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports clinical evaluation by identifying and appraising literature for safety and performance, and by checking recall information for similar devices in FDA, MFDS and MHRA sources.
keyPoints: The device is classified as Class IIb in accordance with Medical Device Regulation (EU) 2017/745 Annex VIII Rule 10.
The document states the device has not been marketed or sold yet in the EU.
The literature search period was 2000.01 to 2022.12, with search date 2023.09 to 2023.10.
The report searched PubMed, European Radiology and Medical Physics and finally selected 16 documents.
Recall searches found 10 FDA MQB product-code results, 0 MFDS results for 의료영상획득장치, and 0 MHRA results for x-ray detector.
The appraisal concluded there were no recalls related to the products and that the device has adequate performance and effectiveness for patients.

evidence.claim: The PICO population includes adults other than pregnant women and children, with hospital use by radiologists or medical imaging specialists.
evidence.evidenceText: The Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector is indicated for diagnosing the adult (not pregnant woman) and children. This product is intended for radiologist or medical imaging specialists who is completed a healthcare professional training. An appropriately trained healthcare professionals can have operator qualifications. The device use settings are intended in hospital.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Intended Population

evidence.claim: The intervention is digital radiography and comparators are computed radiography and conventional radiography.
evidence.evidenceText: Intervention Digital Radiography Comparator Computed Radiography Conventional Radiography (Screen-Film Radiography, Film-Screen Radiography)

evidence.claim: Outcomes include resolution and fast image acquisition time.
evidence.evidenceText: Outcome Resolution (Detective Quantum Efficiency (DQE), Modulation Transfer Function (MTF), etc.) Fast image acquisition time (Image acquisition time, Recovery time, etc.)
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Performance Outcomes

evidence.claim: The planned literature search period was January 2000 through December 2022.
evidence.evidenceText: Limits for start and end dates of each search 2000.01 ~ 2022.12

evidence.claim: The protocol should be updated at least every 5 years.
evidence.evidenceText: This protocol should be updated at least every 5 years to reflect recent publications and technical trends.
evidence.pageTarget: Clinical Evaluation Literature Search Protocol Update

evidence.claim: The literature search date was September 2023 to October 2023.
evidence.evidenceText: Search Date 2023.09 ~ 2023.10

evidence.claim: The literature searcher was Jimin Han.
evidence.evidenceText: Literature searcher Jimin Han

evidence.claim: The report finally selected 16 documents from PubMed, European Radiology and Medical Physics searches.
evidence.evidenceText: We searched in PubMed, European Radiology and Medical Physics as the keywords and finally selected 16 documents.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Literature Appraisal

evidence.claim: The report states clinical data weighting was according to relevance and scientific validity.
evidence.evidenceText: Among these collected data we have weighted according to its relevance to the scope and objectives of the clinical evaluation for the subject devices and scientific validity.

evidence.claim: Literature with total points of 17 to 22 is sufficient for scientific literature databases.
evidence.evidenceText: 17~22 point: Sufficient for scientific Literature databases

evidence.claim: Literature with total points of 13 to 16 is not enough for scientific literature databases but available for clinical databases.
evidence.evidenceText: 13~16 point: Not enough for scientific Literature databases, but available for clinical databases

evidence.claim: Literature with total points of 9 to 12 is inappropriate for clinical evaluation.
evidence.evidenceText: 9~12 point: Inappropriate for clinical evaluation

evidence.claim: The report includes literatures with a score of 14 or higher, but also states to exclude literatures with a score of 14 or less.
evidence.evidenceText: Include literatures with a score of 14 or higher when evaluated according to the tables below... Exclude literatures with a score of 14 or less when evaluated according to the tables below

evidence.claim: Sixteen selected literatures were appraised, with total scores ranging from 14 to 21.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Literature Appraisal

evidence.claim: Equivalent devices for literature review are listed as DRX-1, CXDI-70C and CXDI-31.
evidence.evidenceText: Equivalent device DRX-1 CXDI-70C CXDI-31
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Equivalent Devices

evidence.claim: The state of the art is digital radiography using flat panel detector.
evidence.evidenceText: The state of art is digital radiography using flat panel detector.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Radiography State of the Art

evidence.claim: Alternative treatment options are computed radiography using imaging plate and conventional radiography using X-ray film.
evidence.evidenceText: The alternative treatment options of computed radiography using imaging plate and conventional radiography (screen-film radiography, film-screen radiography) using X-ray film were indicated on below table.

evidence.claim: FDA recall search found 10 MQB product-code recall results for the period 01/01/2020 to 12/12/2022.
evidence.evidenceText: FDA Recall 10 Advance search: 01/01/2020 to 12/12/2022 Keyword: MQB (Product Code)

evidence.claim: MFDS recall search found no recall cases for 의료영상획득장치.
evidence.evidenceText: Korea - MFDS Recall and suspension of sale 0 Advance search: No restrictions. Keyword: 의료영상획득장치 As a result of searching recall information to MFDS with keywords, there were no cases of recall.
evidence.pageTarget: MFDS Medical Imaging Acquisition Device Recall Search

evidence.claim: MHRA recall search found no cases for x-ray detector from 01/01/2020 to 12/12/2022.
evidence.evidenceText: United Kingdom - MHRA Alerts and recalls for drugs and medical devices 0 Advance search: 01/01/2020 to 12/12/2022 Keyword: x-ray detector

evidence.claim: The report concluded that no recalls were related to the products.
evidence.evidenceText: We investigated recalls of similar products in markets such as Korea, the United States, and the United Kingdom, but there were no recalls related to our products.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Recall Appraisal

evidence.claim: The report concluded the device has adequate performance and effectiveness for patients.
evidence.evidenceText: Therefore, we can prove that our device has adequate performance and effectiveness for patients.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Clinical Evaluation Conclusion

reviewFlags.reason: Authority metadata signals MFDS/domestic, while the document text primarily identifies MDR classification and also uses FDA, MFDS and MHRA recall sources; jurisdictional purpose may need confirmation.
Revision value rev.2 is taken from the source filename/path; the visible source text does not show a revision history table or revision number.
The selection criteria contain conflicting threshold wording: include score of 14 or higher, but exclude score of 14 or less.
Several literature appraisal rows contain N/A patient group values while still assigning total scores and declaring the literature sufficient.
The document date is supported by prepared/reviewed/approved dates, but no separate effective date is shown.
